
Film & Zine Afloat: Sip & Swap + Roving Foliofilm-and-zine-afloat-2026
stagnant
review block
✅ CB Matched
soft dawn grain, matte mini‑prints, postage‑style stamps on zines, linen totes, enamel pins · November 2026
A vacation‑first analog photo and mini‑zine vibe with a tiny ‘leave‑one‑take‑one’ swap at Sip and a host‑tended roving folio. Browsing welcome; please leave one before you take one (hosts seed starters for first‑timers). Phones/Instax/disposables/point‑and‑shoots all welcome. Consent is printed and simple: ask first, opt‑in portraits only, no candid strangers, no photographing crew on duty, step away if declined. PG‑13 in public spaces.
Rhapsody of The Seas
From $608/pp · CB_GROUP_INVENTORY
155% price advantage
Booking link: https://bookings.cbagenttools.com/swift/cruise/package/50814?siid=1049337
View JSON
Re-Review
Revise
Remove

Research Intelligence
Data Signals
▸
Instagram #filmphotography: 20M+ posts (2024‑10)
▸
TikTok #35mm/#filmphotography: 1B+ views (2024‑10)
▸
r/analog: ~1M members (2024‑10)
▸
Printed Matter NYABF: ~30k attendees
Why This Niche
Strong analog/zine interest: IG #filmphotography 20M+ posts (2024‑10); TikTok #35mm/#filmphotography 1B+ views; r/analog ~1M; PMABF ~30k attendees. Channels: IG lab/shop collabs, Reddit promoted per mod rules, zine‑fair QR.

Success Logic
Ambient, seated micro‑moments fit small Virgin venues, keeping staff and egress happy while giving guests tangible souvenirs. Clear consent + reciprocity protects vibes and inventory. Off‑peak timing and wave caps prevent crowding; adults‑only setting skews toward guests who already spend on niche hobbies and small art objects.

Vacation Fit
Stamp a postcard, trade a mini‑zine, enjoy a spritz, and drift—casual keepsake‑making that layers lightly onto a sea‑day rhythm without schedules or deliverables.

Niche Expression Mode
Small pins, tiny stamped swaps, and a roving folio—quiet social signals that stay secondary to the vacation.

Community Fit
A gentle leave‑one/take‑one ritual lowers the bar for joining conversation; small artifacts create natural icebreakers while the cruise provides built‑in downtime to browse and share without performance pressure.

Optionality Style
Everything is drop‑in and step‑away friendly. Browse without swapping, or take a starter card and return later. Buddy pairing on request; no critiques, no deadlines, no RSVP.

Cruise-Native Moments
▸
A spritz at Sip while stamping the back of a 4×6 before sail‑away vibes pick up.
▸
Handing a tiny zine to the host’s folio and picking a surprise later.
▸
Window‑light porthole pairs along the Dock House bay without blocking traffic.
▸
A quick ‘voyage postmark’ at the mat before heading to the pool.
▸
Shaded lounger zine flips during a sea‑day breeze.
Optional Gathering Moments
▸
Embark coffee: pin + etiquette card + optional starter kit.
▸
Off‑peak Sip & Swap seatings with 10–12 minute waves.
▸
Roving folio check‑ins during sea days (host announces nearby, then moves on).
▸
Porthole pairs stroll on Deck 7 promenade/Dock House windows (≤6).
▸
Leeward portraits pop‑in (roving; no lines; 1–2 minutes per guest).
Allowed Theme Signals
▸
Anti‑elitism: phones/Instax/disposables/point‑and‑shoots get equal billing; no gear shaming; beginner prompts available.
▸
Flow etiquette printed: 6‑ft buffer at elevator landings; single‑file/keep right; stay seated; avoid blocking bar queues, gangways, or narrow passages; yield to crew/guests.
▸
Consent (printed): opt‑in portraits only; ask before photographing people; no candid strangers; no photographing crew on duty; step away immediately if declined.
▸
Zine folio policy: PG‑13 only in public; 18+ items sealed/labeled for cabin‑only; host pre‑screens and may remove; sanitizer before browsing; folio attended only.
▸
Stamp kit: silicone mat + quick‑dry ink; stamp only on personal paper over the mat—never on ship menus/napkins/wood surfaces; hand wipes provided.
▸
Pin pickup only at embark coffee; stickers guest‑kept; zero adhesives on ship property beyond personal items you own/control. Starter kit (blank postcard + prompt) available at pin pickup to lower friction. Accessibility: seated browsing always available; host can bring the folio to your seat on request. Packing: wrist straps; flash‑off after dusk/indoors.
Solitude Risks
▸
Inventory drain if reciprocity is not enforced or hosts forget to seed starters
▸
Portraits drifting into mini‑shoots without host guardrails
▸
Route creep into narrow corridors if venue limits are not repeated
▸
Folio crowding if waves exceed 6 or dwell times overrun
Discouraged Theme Signals
▸
Standing clusters near elevator landings, gangways, or bar queues
▸
Moving furniture or consolidating tables
▸
Tripods/stands/lights in walkways; blocking egress
▸
Flash after dusk/indoors; photographing crew on duty
▸
Lines for portraits or mini‑shoot setups; posing direction or critique circles
▸
Sales, donations, or brand activations in public venues No pausing in cabin corridors or at stair/elevator landings Leaving the folio unattended in public
Implausible Literalizations
▸
Darkroom builds
▸
Sales booths or paid critiques
▸
Cabin‑door galleries or door drops
▸
Shipwide scavenger hunts with deliverables
▸
Any projector or formal show
Red-Team Suggestions
Stored discovery review for this blueprint.

block
Recommendation
Hold. Do not proceed to Phase 2 until ship/venue alignment is corrected to Rhapsody of the Seas and family-ship consent/PG-13 safeguards are added across the blueprint fields. Operator follow-ups outside the discovery blueprint contract: Retarget blueprint to the matched ship: update shipTarget to “Royal Caribbean Rhapsody of the Seas (Vision class)” and replace all venue/deck references (Sip, Dock House, Deck 7 promenade) with Rhapsody spaces (e.g., Schooner Bar two-tops, Café Latte‑tudes/Latte‑tudes, Boleros quiet corner; outdoor Deck 5 promenade or other wide deck path). Remove adults-only mentions and adjust seat caps/wave notes to chosen venues..

Required Fixes
▸
Rewrite highlightEvents, cruiseNativeMoments, and optionalGatheringMoments to matched-ship spaces with capacities and egress notes (e.g., Sip & Swap at Schooner Bar two‑tops with up to 2 stools; fallback Boleros or a quiet Centrum-adjacent nook; window-light pairs along Deck 5 outer promenade windows). Ensure single-file/keep-right and no blocking near Centrum stairs/elevators.
▸
Remove adults-only framing across successLogic/communityFitRationale. Add explicit minors safeguards to allowedThemeSignals/description/discouragedThemeSignals: no photographing minors; portraits of minors only with their legal guardian’s explicit opt-in and present/nearby; avoid family activity zones; maintain PG-13 in public; hosts may decline any item/photo not suitable for mixed-age spaces.
▸
Resolve the cruise-line conflict between Virgin Voyages Scarlet/Valiant-class (adults-only) and Rhapsody of The Seas before Phase 2.
▸
Remove Virgin-only venue and adults-only framing unless the matched ship is actually a Virgin vessel.
▸
Align shipTarget to the matched inventory: set to Royal Caribbean Rhapsody of the Seas (Vision class) and remove adults-only framing across the blueprint.
▸
Replace Virgin-only venues/routes with Rhapsody-specific spaces across highlightEvents, cruiseNativeMoments, optionalGatheringMoments, and discouragedThemeSignals (e.g., Schooner Bar/Café Latte‑tudes/Boleros nooks; Deck 5 outer promenade or another wide deck path). Adjust seat caps/waves to the chosen venues and restate egress buffers relevant to Centrum/elevators.
▸
Rewrite “Porthole pairs” and any Dock House/Sip mentions to matched-ship locations and correct deck numbers; keep ≤6, single-file/keep-right, and no blocking of stairs/elevator landings.
▸
Add minors-specific consent and family-appropriate content rules to allowedThemeSignals/description/discouragedThemeSignals (no photographing minors; minors’ portraits only with guardian present and explicit opt-in; PG-13 in public; hosts may decline unsuitable items).
Optional Improvements
▸
Scope “stay seated” etiquette to Sip seatings and define folio browsing as host-managed standing only in wide areas or seat-side; auto-reduce wave size if space tightens.
▸
Add ink-handling details: closed, water-based pads; cap between uses; indoor, seated stamping only; avoid linens/upholstery; host may suspend stamping during motion.
▸
State minimal host coverage/sequence within highlightEvents (e.g., 1 host for Sip waves, 1 for folio; if single host, sequence rather than parallel).
▸
Rephrase “Porthole pairs” to a venue-agnostic “Window-light pairs” and reference specific Rhapsody window areas once selected.
▸
Note that outdoor promenade moments do not include stamping; keep stamping at indoor seated tables only.
▸
Retarget blueprint to the matched ship: update shipTarget to “Royal Caribbean Rhapsody of the Seas (Vision class)” and replace all venue/deck references (Sip, Dock House, Deck 7 promenade) with Rhapsody spaces (e.g., Schooner Bar two-tops, Café Latte‑tudes/Latte‑tudes, Boleros quiet corner; outdoor Deck 5 promenade or other wide deck path). Remove adults-only mentions and adjust seat caps/wave notes to chosen venues.
Key Issues
Blueprint cruise line conflicts with the matched ship line
warning
Rewrite ship and line assumptions around Rhapsody of The Seas or intentionally broaden the blueprint to line-agnostic language before Phase 2.
Virgin-specific venue language conflicts with the matched ship
warning
Replace Virgin-only venue and audience framing with ship-accurate or line-agnostic language before Phase 2.
Nonexistent venues/routes create impossible wayfinding on Rhapsody
blocker
Rewrite highlightEvents, cruiseNativeMoments, and optionalGatheringMoments to matched-ship spaces with capacities and egress notes (e.g., Sip & Swap at Schooner Bar two‑tops with up to 2 stools; fallback Boleros or a quiet Centrum-adjacent nook; window-light pairs along Deck 5 outer promenade windows). Ensure single-file/keep-right and no blocking near Centrum stairs/elevators.
Adults-only assumptions conflict with family ship; consent must address minors
blocker
Remove adults-only framing across successLogic/communityFitRationale. Add explicit minors safeguards to allowedThemeSignals/description/discouragedThemeSignals: no photographing minors; portraits of minors only with their legal guardian’s explicit opt-in and present/nearby; avoid family activity zones; maintain PG-13 in public; hosts may decline any item/photo not suitable for mixed-age spaces.
Iteration Status
Next action: branch

Reviews: 3 · Revisions: 1

Deck Sketchbook Society: Off‑Lane Loopsdeck-sketchbook-society-2026
stagnant
review block
✅ CB Matched
slow laps, horizon lines, pencil rolls, sun hats, silicone mats (lap‑only), clean whites and sea blues · November 2026
Pocket‑kit sketching with the sea as your moving subject. Two gentle meet windows daily with parallel off‑lane bays along Deck 5—no stopping in the active lane, no classes, just breezy lines and easy conversation. Printed loop card, join anytime, or skip without FOMO.
Brilliance of the Seas
From $856/pp · CB_GROUP_INVENTORY
19% price advantage
Booking link: https://bookings.cbagenttools.com/swift/cruise/package/42710?siid=1049337
View JSON
Re-Review
Revise
Remove

Research Intelligence
Data Signals
▸
Sonar SNR-26-ART-UX-1372 (2026-02-28): +19% YoY creator-post volume for ‘urban sketching/travel journaling’; median ER 4.8% (n≈6.2k posts).
▸
Sonar SNR-26-HT-URB-SK (2026-02): sustained Q/Q view adds for #urbansketching/#sketchbooktour; 61% of engaged audience aged 25–44.
▸
Meetup/FB Events scrape SNR-26-IRL-015: 50+ public sketchwalks logged Jan–Feb 2026; typical RSVPs 20–120; recurring monthly cadence in 10+ cities.
▸
Google Trends GT-26-URB-KIT (US, 2025): ‘urban sketching kit’ query up ~28% YoY; purchase-intent proxy for travel-sized gear.
Why This Niche
Sonar cohort flags sustained interest and IRL behavior: Sonar ID SNR-26-ART-UX-1372 (refreshed 2026-02-28) shows +19% YoY creator-post volume for ‘urban sketching/travel journaling’ 2025→2026 across IG Reels/TT Shorts (n≈6.2k posts; median ER 4.8%). TikTok hashtag panel SNR-26-HT-URB-SK (2026-02) shows continued quarterly view adds for #urbansketching/#sketchbooktour; growth concentrated 25–44. IRL proof: Meetup/FB public events scrape SNR-26-IRL-015 logs 50+ city sketchwalks Jan–Feb 2026 with 20–120 RSVPs typical. Google Trends GT-26-URB-KIT shows ‘urban sketching kit’ up ~28% YoY (purchase intent proxy). TAM≈0.9–1.3M English-speaking hobbyists; SAM≈180–260k (fly-to-Florida, passport-ready). Reachable engaged audience≈55–85k. Cabin conversion 0.15–0.35% → 80–300 cabins; discovery goal 20–40; floor 8.

Success Logic
Urban sketchers already self-organize as walk‑and‑draw pods; a cruise’s Deck 5 wraparound track turns that habit into relaxed micro‑moments between beaches, shows, and naps. Off‑lane bays + staggered windows keep it safe and frictionless. This audience already spends on compact travel kits and experience-led trips that yield pages and stories; the cruise adds effortless scenery and social overlap without the pressure of a workshop.

Vacation Fit
Two short windows a day fold neatly around pool time, ports, and shows. Off‑lane bays avoid lane conflicts; named indoor/outdoor nooks make wind/rain pivots effortless. No venue bookings and no classes—just join the bay nearest you and drift when you’re done.

Niche Expression Mode
A small pin/sticker and a pocket sketchbook are your only ‘signals.’ The lanyard loop card quietly lists windows, bays, and fallbacks with a tiny bay diagram. Join for a few minutes, wave a page, then drift.

Community Fit
Short, quiet pauses naturally spark tool/page chat, then everyone drifts. Off‑lane single‑file keeps things calm and non‑performative so all skill levels feel welcome without judgement.

Optionality Style
Offline‑first via a bilingual EN/ES loop card with a simple bay diagram and rules. Windows, not fixed starts (15–20 minutes). Three parallel bays per window. Rolling join/exit. Roaming host ratio target 1:12. Per‑bay cap 8; if full, drift to another bay or Promenade A—no queues. Safety code: single‑file along rail; keep inner lane fully open; one hand free while moving; no items on track surface/edge; mats are lap‑only at seated fallbacks; no glass; tethers on tools; pack‑out drips/micro‑trash; quick drip check before leaving. Fallbacks are named and elevator‑close (Promenade A, Central Park A, Boardwalk A). Unaffiliated with Urban Sketchers (USk). Chat is optional; connectivity costs noted. Inclusion: no‑critique/no‑gear‑shame printed. Accessibility: elevator‑close A/C option listed; offer seating first on request.

Cruise-Native Moments
▸
Single‑file rail studies from Bay B while runners glide past and the wake pulls your horizon line.
▸
Aft Port Bay A silhouettes as the ship eases away, then everyone peels off for dinner.
▸
Promenade A rainy‑day reflections across Café Promenade mugs—AC, stools, elevator‑close.
▸
Central Park benches by Trellis Bar for leafy ink textures during a hot midday lull.
Optional Gathering Moments
▸
AM Off‑Lane Loop Window 08:00–08:20: choose Bay A/B/C; roll in/out. If full, float to the next bay.
▸
Golden Rail Studies 17:20–17:40: same bays; roaming hosts keep flow; optional ‘Quiet Flip’ happens at Promenade A for those who prefer seats/AC.
▸
Wind/Rain grid (printed): wind ≥20 kt or rain → Promenade A (Café Promenade seating row, Deck 5, midship elevators); hot sun → Central Park A (benches by Trellis Bar, Deck 8, midship elevators); port‑day crowding → Boardwalk A (aft shade under zip line, Deck 6, aft elevators).
▸
Pocket Tip Envelope Swap: host‑held, in motion during first micro‑stop; no clustering, no unattended items.
▸
Companion‑friendly: partners can stroll alongside but no over‑shoulder filming without consent.
Allowed Theme Signals
▸
Pocket kits only: pencil, fineliner, small watercolor tin, water brush, 5×7 pad.
▸
Water brushes preferred; screw‑lid cups half‑filled if needed; no open jars.
▸
Silicone/kraft mini‑mats allowed only on laps when seated at benches/tables in fallbacks (never on the track).
▸
Rubber‑tipped clips; tool tethers/leashes for caps; carabiner for kits.
▸
Optional micro‑kit loaners (6) in waterproof pouches at Golden window for first‑timers.
Solitude Risks
▸
If bays hit cap without clear overflow, people may feel rejected—mitigate with three parallel bays + Promenade A overflow noted on card.
▸
If hosts don’t roam, pods could harden—mitigate with explicit 1:12 roaming duty and bay sequence A→B→C.
▸
If rules feel fussy, the vibe could turn ‘workshoppy’—mitigate with light iconography and minimal text; keep everything optional.
▸
High wind/rain without a clear grid could scatter the group—mitigate with the printed decision grid and named fallbacks.
Discouraged Theme Signals
▸
No stopping in the active jogging lane at any time.
▸
No items on the Deck 5 track surface or edge (no bags, mats, cups, or boards on the deck).
▸
No shoulder‑to‑shoulder ‘walls’; always single‑file along the rail in bays.
▸
No blades/solvents/aerosols/sprays; no glass containers.
▸
No unattended props, drop boxes, or seat saving; no filming faces/pages without consent.
Implausible Literalizations
▸
Instructor‑led lessons, critiques, or demos.
▸
Cones/signage or reserving public seats/tables.
▸
Tripods/easels or taped ‘stations’ on walkways.
▸
PA announcements or roll calls.
▸
Merch sales tables or gear check‑outs on deck.
Red-Team Suggestions
Stored discovery review for this blueprint.

block
Recommendation
Hold in discovery. Reconcile all ship/class references and locations to Brilliance of the Seas (Radiance‑class) and reframe the route as a Deck 5 Promenade walkway program with Radiance‑appropriate bays and elevator‑close fallbacks. After copy alignment is corrected and motion rules are re‑expressed for a promenade (not an Oasis jogging track), this is ready to proceed to Phase 2. Operator follow-ups outside the discovery blueprint contract: Align to Brilliance/Radiance-class: set shipTarget to Radiance-class (Brilliance OTS) and replace all Oasis-only venues with Radiance-appropriate, elevator-close locations (e.g., Deck 5 Promenade Alcove A/B/C; Centrum seating ring A as indoor fallback). Purge ‘Central Park/Boardwalk/Royal Promenade’ and any Oasis track language..

Required Fixes
▸
Remove Oasis-only venue references unless the matched ship is actually Oasis-class.
▸
Remove wraparound-track assumptions and rebuild the flow around real spaces on brilliance of the seas.
▸
Reconcile ship/class: set shipTarget to Radiance-class (Brilliance of the Seas) and remove all Oasis-only venue names; update HighlightEvents, OptionalGatheringMoments, cruiseNativeMoments, and wind/rain/heat grid to Radiance‑appropriate, elevator‑close locations using ship‑agnostic labels (e.g., Promenade Window Row A/B/C; Centrum Seating A).
▸
Replace ‘Deck 5 jogging track/runners’ framing with ‘Deck 5 Promenade walkway’ language; keep the passing lane fully open, single‑file along the rail, and no stopping in the active walkway lane.
▸
Rename the three bays to plausible Radiance‑class promenade bays (A/B/C) with location descriptors and reassert 1 m/3 ft clearance; adjust copy diagrams/labels on the loop-card description to match.
Optional Improvements
▸
Add explicit ‘no leaning on/over rail’ language to OptionalityStyle/DiscouragedThemeSignals.
▸
Lower default per‑bay cap to 6 on Radiance promenade and publish a simple surge clause (temporary Bay D/E or capped indoor overflow) while maintaining the 1:12 roaming host target.
▸
Standardize the ‘Golden’ window naming and ensure the micro‑kit loaner note references the same label.
▸
Offer a large‑print loop card variant; confirm EN/ES brevity fits the card without ambiguity.
▸
Add a one‑liner that bays may shift ±10–20 m on crew direction; hosts will reposition accordingly.
▸
Align to Brilliance/Radiance-class: set shipTarget to Radiance-class (Brilliance OTS) and replace all Oasis-only venues with Radiance-appropriate, elevator-close locations (e.g., Deck 5 Promenade Alcove A/B/C; Centrum seating ring A as indoor fallback). Purge ‘Central Park/Boardwalk/Royal Promenade’ and any Oasis track language.
Key Issues
Oasis-class venue references do not fit the matched ship
warning
Replace Oasis-only venue references with spaces that actually exist on the matched ship before Phase 2.
Track-layout assumptions do not fit the matched ship
warning
Rewrite circulation and gathering mechanics around spaces that actually exist on brilliance of the seas.
Route relies on an Oasis Deck 5 jogging track that does not exist on Brilliance
warning
Reframe as a Deck 5 Promenade walkway format on Radiance-class: single-file along rail, passing lane kept clear, bays defined as widened promenade recesses/alcoves with 1 m/3 ft clearance; remove ‘jogging track/runners’ language.
Fallback names reference venues not on Brilliance
warning
Rename fallbacks to Radiance‑appropriate, elevator‑close options (e.g., Promenade Window Row A – Deck 5 midship elevators; Centrum Seating Ring A – Deck 4/5 elevators; Aft Promenade Shade A – Deck 5 aft). Keep labels ship‑generic and remove Oasis‑exclusive terms.
Iteration Status
Next action: branch
Stagnation detected: The latest revision is too similar to the previous reviewed version.
Reviews: 8 · Revisions: 3


What is working:

Discovery is no longer blindly advancing bad candidates.
Matching is surfacing real ship constraints instead of hiding them.
Review is now correctly blocking ship-invalid blueprints instead of falsely passing them.
The process is catching structural mismatch before Phase 2, which is exactly what it should do.
What is not working yet:

Revision is still not strong enough to escape bad local minima.
“Branch” mode is being recommended, but the branches are not materially different enough, so the loop stagnates instead of truly rewriting around the matched ship.
In other words, the system can now detect failure well, but it still cannot consistently repair failure well.
The evidence is in the iteration state, not just the campaign copy:

Film is at review block with Next action: branch after limited iteration, which means the gate is functioning.
See WORK.txt:164 and WORK.txt:166.
Deck is also at review block with Next action: branch after many more cycles, which means the repair loop is still recycling instead of producing a genuinely new valid candidate.
See WORK.txt:318 and WORK.txt:320.
So the right diagnosis is:

The system is not broken in the old way.
The system is incomplete in the next way.
Before, the danger was false positives: bad campaigns passing.
Now, the danger is low-yield refinement: bad campaigns are correctly blocked, but the rewrite engine is not yet reliably turning them into good ones.

That is progress. It means the foundation is moving in the right direction. But if your standard is “the process should autonomously produce successful campaigns,” then no, it is not there yet.

The next thing that needs fixing is not discovery matching or review accuracy. It is branch revision quality:

Force branch revisions to change the operating format, not just wording.
Penalize reuse of invalid venue/layout structures from prior iterations.
Make matched-ship constraints first-class inputs to revision candidate generation, not just review-time criticism.
So yes, I understand: you are evaluating the campaign-production machine. My read is that the machine now rejects bad output better than before, but it still does not transform bad output into good output reliably enough to call the overall process successful yet.
